Name: Conditional Access Policy that does not require MFA when sign-in risk has been identified
Description:
This indicator checks whether a Conditional Access policy exists that requires MFA if the authentication request risk is medium or high as determined by Entra ID Protection.

Required permissions

  • Policy.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
Entra ID Protection uses several signals to determine the risk level when a user authenticates to Entra ID. This risk level on sign-in can be used to evaluate whether the authentication event occurring is genuine or malicious.

An attacker may compromise a user account in different ways, either by trying to authenticate as the user with a stolen password, or perform actions such as token replay. These attacks can generate a risk level in Entra ID Protection, that can be used in Conditional Access to force a request for MFA, which can prevent the user from leveraging the stolen credentials to access organizational resources.

References

What are risks in Microsoft Entra ID Protection - Microsoft Entra ID Protection | Microsoft Learn
ResultMessage: There are no Conditional Access Policies configured to require MFA if sign-in risk is detected to be medium or high.
Remediation:
Deploy a Conditional Access policy requiring MFA for risky sign-ins.

Microsoft provides both a Conditional Access policy template, as well as step-by-step instructions for creating a Conditional Access Policy requiring MFA for risky sign-ins.

Implementing changes to Conditional Access policies can negatively impact users if not properly tested. Organizations need to verify all policy and security changes and communicate them out accordingly.
